description_long: This is a short wooden fence section composed of three vertical cylindrical posts with pointed tops, connected by two horizontal rectangular rails. The posts are evenly spaced and the tops are sharpened into conical points, giving the fence a simple yet functional appearance. The wood is a light brown color, with the pointed tips being a slightly lighter shade, suggesting they may be freshly cut or unfinished. The horizontal rails are attached to the posts with visible round fasteners, likely bolts or pegs, adding to the rustic construction. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for marking boundaries or providing a low barrier in urban parks, gardens, or along sidewalks. There are no decorative elements or gates, and the structure appears sturdy but lightweight enough to be moved if necessary.
description: A short, light brown wooden fence with three pointed posts and two horizontal rails, joined by visible fasteners. The design is simple and functional, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing three pointed posts and two horizontal rails, with all fasteners visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cylindrical shape of the posts and the thin profile of the rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the attachment points of the rails to the posts and the uniform spacing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the depth and alignment of the posts.
